div1.entryFree: Subjection, the state of being a subject; service: brought in s. her immortality, and made her thrall to living death, Lucr. 724. (the horse) proud of s., noble by the sway, Compl. 108. his majesty . . . to whom I am now in ward, evermore in s. All's I, 1, 6. to whom I do bequeath my faithful services and true s. everlastingly, John V, 7, 105. whom to disobey were against all proportion of s. H5 IV, 1, 153. I dare be bound he's true and shall perform all parts of his s. loyally, Cymb. IV, 3, 19. I'll tame you, I'll bring you in s. Per. II, 5, 75.
